mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not statistically positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the out-of-sample hit rate falls below 45 percent over a 3-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which accelerates corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in retail sales and housing starts fails to generate positive 60-day returns in at least 60 percent of historical occurrences, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during an economic expansion reflects strengthening supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term positive equity momentum over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return distribution during rising industrial production shows a median return below zero, or the signal loses predictive power when GDP growth exceeds 3 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation reading that exceeds 3.0 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Markets absorb high inflation readings without negative 20-day returns in over 65 percent of cases, or the threshold effect vanishes when real yields remain below 1 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls directly correlates with rising household income and corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next three months.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns in a majority of monthly observations, or the rank correlation between payroll changes and forward returns drops below 0.05 over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms robust aggregate demand, which reduces recession probabilities and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and retail sales signal yields negative 20-day returns in more than half of triggered periods, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production combined with rising unemployment during a contraction regime signals severe output gaps, which accelerates equity sell-offs and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: The dual supply-labor deterioration signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in at least 50 percent of historical contraction periods, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance.
